words.definition_en: not completely accurate but close
words.example: The approximate cost of the renovation will be around $10,000.

words.definition_en: to be almost the same as
words.example: Student numbers are approximating 500 this year.

words.definition_en: to believe or say that something is caused by something else
words.definition_vi: đổ tại, gán cho, quy cho
words.example: He ascribes his success to hard work and luck.

words.definition_en: to give someone a particular job or task
words.example: The teacher assigned a different task to each student.

words.definition_en: to say or think that something is the result of a particular thing
words.example: The doctors attributed his illness to a rare virus.

words.definition_en: an unexpected event that has a damaging effect
words.definition_vi: cú đòn, tai họa bất ngờ
words.example: Losing his job was a terrible blow to his confidence.

words.definition_en: to make something happen
words.example: The heavy rain caused several accidents on the motorway.

words.definition_en: the reason why something happens
words.example: Police are investigating the cause of the explosion.

words.definition_en: an occasion when two or more similar things happen at the same time by chance
words.example: What a coincidence! I was just about to call you.

words.definition_en: to say magic words that are intended to bring bad luck to someone
words.example: The witch cursed the prince, turning him into a frog.

words.definition_en: a magic spell which is intended to harm someone
words.example: Some people believe there is a curse on the family.

words.definition_en: done on purpose rather than by accident
words.example: The attack was a deliberate attempt to destabilize the government.

words.definition_en: to discover the facts about something; to officially decide something
words.example: Tests will determine whether the cancer has spread.

words.definition_en: a power that some people believe controls what will happen in the future
words.example: We still don't know the fate of the missing mountain climbers.

words.definition_en: to change frequently in size, amount, quality, etc.
words.example: Vegetable prices fluctuate according to the season.

words.definition_en: to think or know that something is going to happen in the future
words.example: No one could have foreseen such a dramatic collapse of the stock market.

words.definition_en: something that is very unusual or unexpected
words.definition_vi: điều kỳ quặc, hiện tượng lạ
words.example: The storm was a freak of nature.

words.definition_en: very unusual and unexpected
words.example: He died in a freak accident when a tree fell on his car.

words.definition_en: to risk money on the result of an event
words.example: He gambled all his savings on a business venture that failed.

words.definition_en: not having an obvious order or plan
words.definition_vi: bừa bãi, ngẫu nhiên, không kế hoạch
words.example: The books were piled on the shelves in a haphazard fashion.

words.definition_en: something that is dangerous and likely to cause damage
words.example: Icy roads are a hazard to drivers.

words.definition_en: not intentional; happening by chance
words.example: The government said the border crossing was inadvertent.

words.definition_en: if someone or something is instrumental in causing something to happen, they are one of the most important causes
words.definition_vi: góp phần quyết định, là công cụ
words.example: She was instrumental in bringing about the new law.

words.definition_en: having or bringing bad luck
words.example: The project seemed jinxed from the start.

words.definition_en: the chance that something will happen
words.example: There is every likelihood that the government will win the election.

words.definition_en: an object that is believed to bring good luck
words.example: He never goes to an interview without his lucky charm.

words.definition_en: to flow slowly and change direction frequently (rivers/roads); to wander aimlessly
words.example: The river meanders through the valley.

words.definition_en: an unlucky accident that does not have serious results
words.example: The parade went off without any mishap.

words.definition_en: to develop new physical characteristics because of a permanent change in the genes
words.example: The virus has mutated into a more dangerous form.

words.definition_en: the probability that something will happen
words.example: The odds are that it will rain today.
collocations: the odds are in favor of

words.definition_en: to choose someone or something from a group
words.example: She picked the best apples from the basket.

words.definition_en: the person or thing that has been chosen
words.definition_vi: sự lựa chọn, vật được chọn
words.example: Take your pick – they're all the same price.

words.definition_en: a situation in which you do not know what you will get and you hope that it will be good
words.definition_vi: có gì ăn nấy, sự may rủi
words.example: We didn't have a reservation, so we just took pot luck at the restaurant.

words.definition_en: happening or chosen by chance rather than according to a plan
words.example: We asked a random sample of people for their opinions.

words.definition_en: very small (used about chances)
words.example: There is only a slim chance that the missing plane will be found.

words.definition_en: to guess about the possible causes or effects of something without knowing all the facts
words.example: It's useless to speculate on what might have happened.

words.definition_en: happening or done in a natural, often sudden way, without any planning
words.example: The audience burst into spontaneous applause.

words.definition_en: to surprise a person or animal, often making them move suddenly
words.example: A loud noise startled the horse.

words.definition_en: a collection of facts in the form of numbers
words.example: Statistics show that more people are using the internet than ever before.

words.definition_en: to move away from a place where you should be or from a path that you should follow
words.example: Several of the sheep had strayed from the flock.

words.definition_en: away from the correct place or having no home
words.example: He was hit by a stray bullet during the riot.

words.definition_en: a belief that some objects or actions are lucky or unlucky, based on old ideas of magic
words.example: According to local superstition, it is unlucky to see a black cat.

words.definition_en: believing in superstitions
words.example: I'm not superstitious, but I still don't like walking under ladders.

words.definition_en: to happen; to become known
words.definition_vi: xảy ra, lộ ra, hóa ra
words.example: It later transpired that the information was false.

words.definition_en: a situation in which you are not sure about something
words.definition_vi: sự không chắc chắn, tình trạng bấp bênh
words.example: There is a lot of uncertainty about the future of the company.

words.definition_en: to move from side to side in an unsteady way
words.example: The table wobbles because one leg is shorter than the others.

words.definition_en: the practice or science of farming
words.example: Modern agriculture relies heavily on technology.

words.definition_en: to understand how good or useful someone or something is
words.example: We really appreciate all the help you gave us.

words.definition_en: a sudden event that causes many people to suffer
words.example: The environmental catastrophe was caused by the oil spill.

words.definition_en: a plant such as a grain, fruit, or vegetable that is grown by farmers
words.example: The main crops in this region are wheat and corn.

words.definition_en: a long period of time when there is little or no rain
words.example: The drought caused many animals to die of thirst.

words.definition_en: to send people away from a dangerous place to a safe place
words.example: The police evacuated the building because of a bomb threat.

words.definition_en: to use something fully and effectively; to treat someone unfairly by using their work
words.example: We need to exploit our natural resources more effectively.

words.definition_en: a situation in which many people do not have enough food to eat
words.example: The war led to widespread famine in the region.

words.definition_en: to cover a place with water, or to become covered with water
words.example: The heavy rain flooded the basement.

words.definition_en: a large amount of water that covers an area that was dry
words.example: The village was cut off by the floods.

words.definition_en: fuels such as coal or oil that are produced from the remains of plants and animals
words.example: Burning fossil fuels is a major cause of global warming.

words.definition_en: an increase in the average temperature of the Earth's atmosphere
words.example: Most scientists agree that global warming is caused by human activity.

words.definition_en: green plants or branches
words.example: The room was decorated with flowers and greenery.

words.definition_en: the type of place that a particular animal or plant usually lives in
words.example: The panda's natural habitat is the bamboo forest.

words.definition_en: to collect a crop from the fields
words.example: Farmers are busy harvesting the rice.

words.definition_en: the time when crops are gathered; the amount of crops gathered
words.definition_vi: mùa gặt, sản lượng thu hoạch
words.example: We're hoping for a good harvest this year.

words.definition_en: a violent storm with extremely strong winds
words.example: The hurricane caused widespread damage along the coast.

words.definition_en: a natural tendency to behave in a particular way that people and animals are born with
words.example: Birds build nests by instinct.

words.definition_en: an event such as a flood, earthquake, or hurricane that causes great damage or loss of life
words.example: The earthquake was the worst natural disaster in the country's history.

words.definition_en: something such as useful land, or minerals such as oil or coal, that exists in a country and can be used to increase its wealth
words.example: The country is rich in natural resources.

words.definition_en: if something is scarce, there is not enough of it available
words.example: Food and water were becoming increasingly scarce.

words.definition_en: a group of animals or plants of the same kind that can breed with each other
words.example: There are many species of birds in this forest.

words.definition_en: find or see someone or something when you did not expect to
words.definition_vi: tình cờ thấy, tình cờ gặp
words.example: I chanced upon a very rare book in a second-hand shop.

words.definition_en: meet someone or find something by chance
words.example: I came across my old school photos while I was tidying the attic.

words.definition_en: become cooler, or make something cooler
words.example: We waited for the soup to cool down before eating it.

words.definition_en: reduce the amount of something, especially money that you spend
words.example: The government is trying to cut back on public spending.

words.definition_en: become weaker or less common and then disappear completely
words.example: Dinosaurs died out millions of years ago.

words.definition_en: remove something from under the ground by digging; find information by searching carefully
words.example: The police are digging up the garden to look for the body.

words.definition_en: if something dries up, all the water comes out of it; stop being available
words.example: The river dries up every summer. Funds for the project have dried up.

words.definition_en: use or finish something; manage to deal with a difficult situation; finish dealing with work
words.definition_vi: hoàn thành, vượt qua, dùng hết
words.example: We got through a huge amount of work today.

words.definition_en: make something hot; become hot
words.example: The competition is starting to heat up.

words.definition_en: destroy living things so that most or all of them are dead
words.example: Pollution is killing off the fish in the river.

words.definition_en: if you put something down to a particular reason, you think it has happened for that reason
words.example: I put his bad mood down to tiredness.

words.definition_en: make a careless mistake
words.example: Someone slipped up and sent the wrong letter.

words.definition_en: appear or be produced suddenly and quickly
words.definition_vi: mọc lên như nấm, xuất hiện bất ngờ
words.example: Fast-food restaurants are springing up all over the city.

words.definition_en: keep a lot of something so that you can use it later; do something that will cause problems in the future
words.example: You're just storing up trouble for yourself in the future.

words.definition_en: get rid of something; force someone to leave; refuse to accept a plan
words.definition_vi: vứt bỏ, đuổi đi, bác bỏ
words.example: They threw out the proposal without even looking at it.

words.definition_en: produce something new or unexpected; cause dust or water to rise into the air
words.definition_vi: gợi lên, làm nảy sinh, nôn mửa
words.example: The investigation has thrown up some interesting new facts.

words.definition_en: risk money on the result of an event
words.example: I bet $20 on that horse.

words.definition_en: having no doubt
words.example: I am certain that he is lying.

words.definition_en: the possibility of something happening
words.example: Is there any chance of getting a ticket?

words.definition_en: effort or power
words.example: He succeeded by dint of hard work.

words.definition_en: the act of getting rid of something
words.definition_vi: sự vứt bỏ, sự tùy ý sử dụng
words.example: The waste disposal system is broken.

words.definition_en: to discover something
words.example: I find it difficult to believe her.

words.definition_en: relating to protection of the environment
words.example: The government is trying to promote green politics.

words.definition_en: give an answer without being sure
words.example: Take a guess at how much it cost.

words.definition_en: to take place
words.example: It so happens that I'm going that way myself.

words.definition_en: a flash of bright light in the sky during a storm
words.example: The tree was struck by lightning.

words.definition_en: good things that happen to you by chance
words.example: Wish me luck!

words.definition_en: existing in nature
words.example: Death was from natural causes.

words.definition_en: the physical world; the character of a person
words.example: It's not in his nature to be mean.

words.definition_en: probability
words.example: The odds of winning are slim.
collocations: the odds are in favour of/against

words.definition_en: water falling from the sky
words.example: It's pouring with rain.

words.definition_en: possibility of something bad happening
words.example: Smoking increases the risk of heart disease.

words.definition_en: the star that the Earth moves around
words.example: We sat in the sun all afternoon.

words.definition_en: a suggestion that something unpleasant will happen
words.example: He faced a death threat.

words.definition_en: atmospheric conditions
words.example: I'm feeling a bit under the weather.

words.definition_en: moving air
words.example: A gust of wind blew my hat off.

words.definition_en: an event such as a flood, lightning or an earthquake that is produced by natural forces, and that people have no control over
words.definition_vi: thiên tai, trường hợp bất khả kháng
words.example: The insurance company refused to pay, claiming the damage was an act of God.

words.definition_en: used for saying that something always happens or someone always does something despite bad weather or difficult conditions
words.definition_vi: dù mưa hay nắng, dù có chuyện gì xảy ra
words.example: He goes jogging every morning, come rain or shine.

words.definition_en: having a long period of difficulty finding work and earning money
words.definition_vi: gặp vận đen, đang lúc khó khăn
words.example: He's been down on his luck since he lost his job last year.

words.definition_en: be chosen to do something unpleasant
words.definition_vi: phải làm việc không ai muốn, bốc trúng lá thăm xấu
words.example: I drew the short straw and had to stay late to finish the report.

words.definition_en: be good at growing plants
words.example: My grandmother has green fingers; her garden is always beautiful.

words.definition_en: allow something to develop without trying to influence it
words.definition_vi: để mọi thứ diễn ra tự nhiên
words.example: There's no cure for this cold; we just have to let nature take its course.

words.definition_en: used for emphasising that there is no reason or explanation for something
words.definition_vi: không có lý do rõ ràng, chẳng vì cớ gì
words.example: There seems to be no rhyme or reason to these new rules.

words.definition_en: happening in a way that is sudden and unexpected
words.example: She received a job offer out of the blue.

words.definition_en: used for saying people have limited control over what life brings them
words.definition_vi: do hên xui, sự may rủi
words.example: In this business, success is often just the luck of the draw.

words.definition_en: used when you have said that you have had good luck in order to prevent bad luck from happening to you
words.definition_vi: trộm vía, cầu may (bằng cách gõ vào gỗ)
words.example: I've never been in a car accident, touch wood.

exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

items.explanation: Foresee means to predict or see the future.
items.sentence: The woman claimed to be able to ___ the future, but I knew she was only guessing.

items.explanation: Gamble means to risk money or everything on a result.
items.sentence: In a moment of madness, I decided to ___ everything on the turn of one card.

items.explanation: Mutate means to undergo a genetic change.
items.sentence: It seems that one of the genes has ___ , causing the frog to be blue.

items.explanation: Attribute (passive: be attributed to) means to say that something is the result of something else.
items.sentence: Following the investigation, the accident was ___ to chance.

items.explanation: Startle means to surprise or frighten someone suddenly.
items.sentence: When the glass fell off the table on its own, it really ___ me.

items.explanation: Fluctuate means to change frequently or go up and down.
items.sentence: A casino's profits might ___ from month to month, but you can be sure they will always make money.

items.explanation: Determine means to discover the official facts or causes.
items.sentence: Police have yet to ___ what caused the building to collapse.

items.explanation: Wobble means to shake or move unsteadily from side to side.
items.sentence: The first I knew of the earthquake was when the furniture started to ___ .

items.explanation: Pick means to choose.
items.sentence: Is there a winning roulette system that can help you ___ the right number?

items.explanation: Meander means to flow in a winding or curved path.
items.sentence: As the river gets closer to the sea, it begins to ___ across the flat lowlands.

exercises.label: A

items.explanation: Approximate means near to the actual number.
items.sentence: What's the ___ number of accidents which happen in the home each year?

items.explanation: A lucky charm is an object believed to bring good luck.
items.sentence: I always take my lucky ___ with me into an exam.

items.explanation: Coincidence is when two things happen by chance at the same time.
items.sentence: Did you plan to meet up with Emma in London, or was it just a ___ ?

items.explanation: Deliberate means done on purpose.
items.sentence: Johnnie broke the vase, but I'm sure it wasn't ___ .

items.explanation: Speculate means to guess without knowing all the facts.
items.sentence: We can only ___ as to the causes of the disaster.

items.explanation: Likelihood refers to the probability of something happening.
items.sentence: The ___ of one winning the lottery is hardly worth even considering.

items.explanation: Pot luck means taking a chance on whatever is available.
items.sentence: I didn't know my guess was going to be right - it was just ___ .

items.explanation: Superstition is a belief based on old ideas of magic or luck.
items.sentence: Do you believe in that old ___ about not walking under a ladder?

exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the boxes.

items.explanation: Spontaneous means happening suddenly without planning.
items.sentence: Have you ever done something completely ___ , like suddenly deciding to quit your job?

items.explanation: Haphazard means lacking any plan or order.
items.sentence: This government doesn't seem to have a plan and is just reacting to events in a ___ manner.

items.explanation: A blow is an unexpected damaging event.
items.sentence: Having an accident in the first race was a real ___ to Johnson's ambitions of becoming world champion.

items.explanation: Mishaps are unlucky accidents.
items.sentence: We should get there by midnight, as long as there are no ___ along the way.

items.explanation: A freak accident is one that is very unusual.
items.sentence: Nobody could have foreseen what would happen - it was just a ___ accident.

items.explanation: Inadvertent means unintentional.
items.sentence: The politician's ___ comments to the press started a national scandal.

items.explanation: Statistics is the science of using number data.
items.sentence: It seems to me that you can prove just about anything with ___ .

items.explanation: The odds are the probability of an outcome.
items.sentence: The ___ are 7-1 against Argentina getting to the final of the competition.

items.explanation: Jinxed means bringing or having bad luck.
items.sentence: Seven people have got ill on the voyage so far; I think the cruise is ___ .

items.explanation: Superstitious people believe in luck/magic controlling events.
items.sentence: Aren't ___ people just looking for a way to control things that are really out of their control?

items.explanation: Assigned means given a specific job or location.
items.sentence: Andy's been lucky enough to be ___ to the Sydney office and he's leaving next week.

items.explanation: Ascribe means to believe something is caused by something else.
items.sentence: Wilson ___ his defeat to bad luck.

items.explanation: A stray bullet is one that has hit something other than the target.
items.sentence: Apparently, the car was hit by a ___ bullet during the riot.

items.explanation: Random means chosen by chance.
items.sentence: Just think of a ___ number and then write it on here.

items.explanation: Cause means to make something happen.
items.sentence: It looks like the fire was ___ by a discarded cigarette.

items.explanation: Transpire means to happen or to become known.
items.sentence: We may never know what ___ that fateful night.

exercises.label: C

exercises.instruction: If the word or phrase in bold is correct, put a tick. If it is incorrect, replace it with a word or phrase in bold from another sentence.

items.explanation: An earthquake is a catastrophe.
items.sentence: The earthquake was a real ___ and destroyed most of the buildings in the city.

items.explanation: You evacuate people from a dangerous area.
items.sentence: They are going to ___ the local people because the volcano might erupt.

items.explanation: Fossil fuels are things like coal and oil that we need alternatives for.
items.sentence: We need to find alternatives to ___ before we destroy our planet.

items.explanation: Greenery refers to plants/nature found in summer.
items.sentence: There's so much ___ in England during the summer.

items.explanation: Habitat is the natural environment of an animal.
items.sentence: The panda is threatened by the destruction of its ___ .

items.explanation: You harvest grapes.
items.sentence: They were planning to ___ the grapes the next day and nobody could have predicted the storm.

items.explanation: Animals act by instinct.
items.sentence: Some animals seem to know by ___ when bad weather is on the way.

items.explanation: Natural resources are useful things from nature like oil.
items.sentence: The oil fields are a vast natural ___ that belongs to all the people, not just a few businessmen.

items.explanation: Scarce means hard to find, which is correct for sparrows here.
items.sentence: The sparrow is becoming increasingly scarce in this area.

items.explanation: A species is a kind or type of animal.
items.sentence: Scientists claim to have identified a new ___ of monkey.

exercises.label: D

exercises.instruction: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.explanation: Agriculture is the practice of farming.
items.sentence: It seems that ___ first developed in the Middle East.

items.explanation: A crop is a specific plant grown for food/profit.
items.sentence: Coffee is a very important ___ in Brazil.

items.explanation: Famine is a widespread lack of food.
items.sentence: The ___ was caused by war in the region.

items.explanation: Drought is a lack of water/rain.
items.sentence: If the ___ continues, the government is planning to introduce a ban on washing cars.

items.explanation: Appreciate means to value or recognize the beauty of something.
items.sentence: You have to stop and get out of your car to really ___ the natural beauty of the area.

items.explanation: Exploit means to use something for profit.
items.sentence: Why do you think that we don't have the right to ___ nature to make money?

items.explanation: Global warming is caused by human activity.
items.sentence: Some scientists still dispute that ___ is caused by human activity.

items.explanation: Natural disasters like floods force people to leave homes.
items.sentence: Over half a million people were forced to leave their homes as a result of the recent ___ .

items.explanation: Constant rain causes floods.
items.sentence: It's been raining for days and there's a real danger of ___ if it doesn't stop soon.

items.explanation: Hurricanes start at sea and move inland.
items.sentence: This area is frequently hit by ___ that start at sea and then move inland.

exercises.label: E

exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.explanation: Put something down to means to attribute it to.
items.sentence: the majority of scientists put it ___ to human activity.

items.explanation: Cut back on means to reduce.
items.sentence: The solution, they claim, is to cut back ___ our consumption of fossil fuels

items.explanation: Heat up means to become hot.
items.sentence: over time the Earth heats ___

items.explanation: Cool down means to become cool.
items.sentence: and cools ___ naturally.

items.explanation: Get through means to use up or finish.
items.sentence: we should just continue to get ___ the fossil fuels

items.explanation: Die out means to become extinct.
items.sentence: many species will die ___ as once fertile areas turn to desert.

items.explanation: Dry up means to lose all water.
items.sentence: rivers will dry ___ in other areas

items.explanation: Kill off means to destroy life completely.
items.sentence: killing ___ animal and plant life that rely on them.

exercises.label: F

exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the phrasal verbs in the box.

items.explanation: Chance upon means to find by accident.
items.sentence: We were walking through the woods when we ___ a trap set by hunters.

items.explanation: Slip up means to make a mistake.
items.sentence: It's a huge risk, and we can't afford to ___ or everything will go wrong.

items.explanation: Throw out means to discard.
items.sentence: Don't just ___ those plastic bottles - we can recycle them.

items.explanation: Store up means to keep for future use.
items.sentence: Isn't it amazing how squirrels ___ nuts to make sure they have enough to eat in the winter?

items.explanation: Throw up means to produce unexpected problems.
items.sentence: The new law has ___ a number of problems for farmers that weren't expected.

items.explanation: Come across means to find by chance.
items.sentence: As I was flicking through the magazine, I ___ an article about the local nature reserve.

items.explanation: Dig up means to find information or evidence by searching.
items.sentence: According to the evidence that has been ___ , people have been farming this area for over a 1,000 years.

items.explanation: Spring up means to appear suddenly.
items.sentence: Organisations concerned about the environment seem to have ___ everywhere these days.

exercises.label: G

exercises.instruction: Put the following phrasal verbs into the sentences in the correct form and tick the sentences where the phrasal verb means something like 'remove'.

items.explanation: Cross off means to remove from a list.
items.sentence: After what Maria said, I think we should ___ her ___ the guest list.

items.explanation: Wipe off means to remove by wiping.
items.sentence: Calvin ___ the chocolate ___ his mouth with the back of his hand.

items.explanation: Shave off means to remove by shaving.
items.sentence: Do you think Dad will ever ___ his beard ___ ?

items.explanation: Go off means to spoil (food). Does not mean remove.
items.sentence: There's a funny smell, like something's ___ in the fridge.

items.explanation: Chop off means to remove by cutting.
items.sentence: Aren't you going to ___ the head ___ the fish before you serve it?

items.explanation: Drop off means to fall asleep. Does not mean remove.
items.sentence: I'm so tired after work that I often just ___ in front of the TV.

items.explanation: Set off means to start a journey. Does not mean remove.
items.sentence: We're going to ___ early in the morning, so try to get some sleep.

items.explanation: Peel off means to remove by peeling.
items.sentence: You need to ___ the paper ___ the top of the ice cream before you eat it.

exercises.label: H

exercises.instruction: Write one preposition in each gap.

items.explanation: Struck by lightning is the standard phrase.
items.sentence: It's a million to one against you being struck ___ lightning.

items.explanation: Caught in the rain is the standard phrase.
items.sentence: We got caught ___ the rain so we were completely soaked when we got home.

items.explanation: By dint of means by means of or because of.
items.sentence: Many people only manage to give up gambling ___ dint of a lot of hard work.

items.explanation: A gust of wind is the collocation.
items.sentence: A sudden gust ___ wind blew the letter out of my hand.

items.explanation: Bet on something.
items.sentence: I can't believe you bet your car ___ the toss of a coin!

items.explanation: Lying in the sun.
items.sentence: I'd love to be a cat and spend all day lying ___ the sun.

items.explanation: In luck means being lucky at a specific moment.
items.sentence: I believe we're ___ luck this time and we won't have lost our money.

items.explanation: Lucky by nature means lucky as a natural trait.
items.sentence: You're either lucky ___ nature or you have to work hard all your life.

exercises.label: I

exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.explanation: Hazard a guess is a common idiom.
items.sentence: If you were to hazard a ___ at when and where the next major earthquake will strike

items.explanation: There's every chance means it is very likely.
items.sentence: there's ___ chance that you'd be almost as accurate as the experts.

items.explanation: Threat posed to someone/something.
items.sentence: considering the threat posed ___ human civilisation.

items.explanation: At their disposal means available for use.
items.sentence: Even with vast resources at ___ disposal

items.explanation: The odds are means the probabilities are.
items.sentence: often the best that scientists can do is say that ___ odds are that an earthquake will strike

items.explanation: Chances of doing something.
items.sentence: One reason the chances ___ making an accurate prediction are so low

items.explanation: The nature of something.
items.sentence: is the nature ___ the forces involved.

items.explanation: Put something at risk.
items.sentence: Scientists need to decide whether to put lives at ___ by not issuing a warning

items.explanation: Threat of something.
items.sentence: or constantly issue warnings about the threat ___ an earthquake

items.explanation: The odds are against us means we are unlikely to succeed.
items.sentence: In the end, the odds seem to be ___ us solving this difficult problem any time soon.

exercises.label: J

exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.explanation: Say for certain.
items.sentence: I think Jay plays poker, but I couldn't ___ for certain.

items.explanation: Natural selection is a biological process.
items.sentence: If one species survives at the expense of another, then I think that's just ___ selection.

items.explanation: Make certain means to ensure.
items.sentence: I'll just call Jimmy to ___ certain he can look after the dogs.

items.explanation: Give the green light means to give permission.
items.sentence: The Minister gave the ___ light for the bypass to go ahead.

items.explanation: Give someone a second chance.
items.sentence: You were a bit hard on him for crashing your car - give him a ___ chance!

items.explanation: Push your luck means to take too many risks.
items.sentence: People are ___ their luck a bit too far when they live on the slopes of a volcano.

items.explanation: Pose a threat means to present a threat.
items.sentence: Global warming will ___ a great threat to mankind in years to come.

items.explanation: Pour with rain means to rain heavily.
items.sentence: Just at the moment we had to go out, it started to ___ with rain.

exercises.label: K

exercises.instruction: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.

items.explanation: Find yourself doing; find something difficult; find your way.
items.sentence: Have you ever ___ yourself wondering what it would be like to be someone else? / I'm surprised you ___ the test so difficult after all that revision. / After wandering in the forest for an hour, Gale finally ___ her way out.

items.explanation: Economic in nature; human nature; calm by nature.
items.sentence: The problem of conservation is essentially economic in ___ . / It's only human ___ to want to acquire more wealth. / I'm quite a calm person by ___ , but things like this make me really angry.

items.explanation: Leave to chance; by any chance; last chance.
items.sentence: Let's go through things again; I don't want to leave anything to ___ . / Excuse me, but are you Mr Whitehouse, by any ___ ? / I mean it - this is your last ___ and next time you make a mistake, you're out.

items.explanation: Under the weather; weather forecast; awful weather.
items.sentence: I haven't been at work all week because I've been a bit under the ___ . / According to the ___ forecast, we're in for thunderstorms. / We've had some really awful ___ lately, haven't we?

items.explanation: As luck would have it; in luck; push your luck.
items.sentence: I was wondering how to get home when, as ___ would have it, Ben drove past in his car. / The assistant said that I was in ___ and that they only had one pair left but they were my size. / You've won by betting on red the last three times, but don't push your ___ !

items.explanation: It so happens; as it happens; freak storm happens (occurs).
items.sentence: It so ___ that I've already decided to do that, anyway. / As it ___ , we can cancel the demonstration because the council have changed their mind. / When a freak storm ___ , we are not always given any warning of it.

items.explanation: Certain amount; certain to find (it will be certain); a certain Mr Pratt (used to refer to someone whose name you know but nothing else).
items.sentence: I have to admit to feeling a ___ amount of fear as I stepped into the lions' cage. / The press are ___ to find out about this, and then it'll be all over the papers. / A ___ Mr Pratt rang this afternoon about your donation to the WWF.

items.explanation: I guess; guess at (someone's age); never guess (predict/believe).
items.sentence: I ___ you must be right about the weather - it feels hot! / Don't ask me to ___ at her age as I'd be miles out. / You'd never ___ they were married from the way they act in public, would you?

exercises.label: L

exercises.instruction: Complete each dialogue using the appropriate form of an idiom in the box.

items.explanation: Down on one's luck means having a difficult period.
items.sentence: Yes, she does seem to have been rather ___ lately, doesn't she?

items.explanation: Let nature take its course means to let things develop naturally.
items.sentence: Yes, I think we should just ___ and see what happens.

items.explanation: Out of the blue means suddenly and unexpectedly.
items.sentence: It must have been a real shock when he turned up ___ after all these years.

items.explanation: Come rain or shine means no matter what the conditions.
items.sentence: Oh, yes. You know him. He's there ___ .

items.explanation: The luck of the draw means it was a matter of chance.
items.sentence: I don't know. It was just ___ , I guess.

items.explanation: An act of God is a natural event beyond human control.
items.sentence: Well, it's not easy to predict that kind of ___ , is it?

items.explanation: No rhyme or reason means no explanation.
items.sentence: Me neither. There seems to be ___ to it.

items.explanation: Draw the short straw means being chosen for an unpleasant task.
items.sentence: Well, I ___ , so I'm staying in while the others go out.

items.explanation: Touch wood is said to avoid bad luck.
items.sentence: The weather should be good, ___ .

items.explanation: Have green fingers means being good at gardening.
items.sentence: Thanks. Yes, I suppose I've always ___ .

exercises.label: M

exercises.instruction: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.explanation: Noun form of conserve.
items.sentence: The World Wide Fund for Nature (WWF) has been involved in ___ (CONSERVE) since its foundation in 1961.

items.explanation: Noun form of intend.
items.sentence: Its ___ (INTEND) has always been to protect

items.explanation: Noun referring to wild animals.
items.sentence: ___ (WILD) against the effects of human activity.

items.explanation: Plural noun for people who study ecology.
items.sentence: Working very closely with both expert ___ (ECOLOGY) and ordinary local people,

items.explanation: Adjective/Participle form of threat.
items.sentence: the organisation aims to help those animals ___ (THREAT) by the destruction of habitats.

items.explanation: Noun form of extinct.
items.sentence: Economic growth and tourism force some species to the brink of ___ (EXTINCT).

items.explanation: Adverb form of fortune.
items.sentence: ___ (FORTUNE), the WWF work hard to ensure

items.explanation: Adverb form of probable.
items.sentence: that animals such as the giant panda will ___ (PROBABLE) survive for a few more years yet.

items.explanation: Adjective meaning too great to be overcome (from mount).
items.sentence: Refusing to accept that the problems are ___ (MOUNT),

items.explanation: Adverb form of seem.
items.sentence: the WWF works tirelessly on a ___ (SEEM) never-ending list of projects

exercises.label: N

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.explanation: Noun form of occur.
items.sentence: The chances against this kind of unusual ___ are very high. OCCUR

items.explanation: Adjective form of cause (relating to cause and effect).
items.sentence: I'm not convinced that there's a ___ link between pollution and global warming. CAUSE

items.explanation: Noun form of erode.
items.sentence: The ___ of the cliffs by the sea has completely changed the landscape. ERODE

items.explanation: Adjective form of instinct.
items.sentence: Female animals have an ___ need to protect their young. INSTINCT

items.explanation: Adjective form of mystery.
items.sentence: The police are investigating the ___ disappearance from the zoo of a number of animals. MYSTERY

items.explanation: Adjective form of risk.
items.sentence: We knew that getting between the whale and the ship was ___ , but it was the only way to stop them hunting. RISK

items.explanation: Noun form of assess.
items.sentence: What's your ___ of the situation? ASSESS

items.explanation: Adjective form of favour.
items.sentence: We have to wait for ___ conditions to launch the boat. FAVOUR

items.explanation: Adjective form of elude.
items.sentence: The expedition travelled miles in search of the ___ mountain gorilla. ELUDE

items.explanation: Noun form of erupt.
items.sentence: Following the ___ of the volcano, hundreds of villages were destroyed. ERUPT

exercises.label: O

exercises.instruction: Tick the words which can have -y added to them to make adjectives. You may need to double a final letter or remove a final -e.

items.explanation: Brain + y = brainy.
items.sentence: brain -> ___

items.explanation: Bump + y = bumpy.
items.sentence: bump -> ___

items.explanation: Curl + y = curly.
items.sentence: curl -> ___

items.explanation: Flavour does not take -y (usually flavourful/flavourless).
items.sentence: flavour -> ___

items.explanation: Guilt + y = guilty.
items.sentence: guilt -> ___

items.explanation: Hair + y = hairy.
items.sentence: hair -> ___

items.explanation: Haste + y (remove e) = hasty.
items.sentence: haste -> ___

items.explanation: Juice + y (remove e) = juicy.
items.sentence: juice -> ___

items.explanation: Luck + y = lucky.
items.sentence: luck -> ___

items.explanation: Lump + y = lumpy.
items.sentence: lump -> ___

items.explanation: Meat + y = meaty.
items.sentence: meat -> ___

items.explanation: Milk + y = milky.
items.sentence: milk -> ___

items.explanation: Scare + y (remove e) = scary.
items.sentence: scare -> ___

items.explanation: Shine + y (remove e) = shiny.
items.sentence: shine -> ___

items.explanation: Sport + y = sporty.
items.sentence: sport -> ___

items.explanation: Spot + y (double t) = spotty.
items.sentence: spot -> ___

items.explanation: Taste + y (remove e) = tasty.
items.sentence: taste -> ___

items.explanation: Waste does not take -y (wasteful).
items.sentence: waste -> ___

items.explanation: Water + y = watery.
items.sentence: water -> ___

items.explanation: Wealth + y = wealthy.
items.sentence: wealth -> ___

exercises.label: P
